environment._NOTE_1_DB: Default DB (recommended): context-index__ollama__nomic-embed-text-v1.5__768.sqlite (auto-derived from provider+model+dim). Pull `ollama pull nomic-embed-text:v1.5` for the local-first new-user setup; hf-local with nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5 is the zero-install Node fallback (no Python).
environment._NOTE_3_PROVIDERS: Auto cascade (ADR-014, local-first): Ollama (local) -> hf-local (pure-Node @huggingface/transformers HTTP model server, zero-install) -> OpenAI API -> Voyage API.
environment._NOTE_4_EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER: Auto cascade (ADR-014, local-first): Ollama -> hf-local -> OpenAI -> Voyage. Set EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER=<provider> (ollama|hf-local|openai|voyage) to force a specific tier.
environment._NOTE_5_CLOUD_PROVIDERS: Cloud tiers are explicit opt-in: add VOYAGE_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY only when you want cloud embeddings; otherwise the cascade keeps everything local. Voyage is embeddings-only here; cloud rerankers (voyage/cohere) were removed in 022/013.
environment._NOTE_8_FEATURE_FLAGS: Opt-out flags (default ON): SPECKIT_ADAPTIVE_FUSION, SPECKIT_SESSION_BOOST, SPECKIT_CAUSAL_BOOST, SPECKIT_HYDE_ACTIVE, SPECKIT_ENTITY_LINKING, SPECKIT_GRAPH_CONCEPT_ROUTING, SPECKIT_GRAPH_REFRESH_MODE=write_local. Tier 3 LLM save routing is always on (fail-open to Tier 2 on timeout).
environment._NOTE_DAEMON_REELECTION: Daemon re-election is on by default in the launcher code: on disposal the launcher releases the shared daemon for a live secondary to adopt instead of killing it, so concurrent sessions keep MCP transport. An unadopted released daemon is bounded by SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN. Set SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION=0 to restore kill-on-disposal. See ENV_REFERENCE.md.
environment._NOTE_HF_EMBED_SOCKET: Shared hf-local model-server socket. Pinned identically for mk-spec-memory AND mk_skill_advisor so both consume ONE resident nomic server (single-owner via the socket-keyed respawn lock). Short /tmp path stays under the macOS sun_path 104-char limit. Do not diverge the two services.
environment._NOTE_RECOMMENDED_SETUP: New users: install Ollama (https://ollama.com) and run `ollama pull nomic-embed-text:v1.5`. The cascade auto-detects Ollama and persists nomic-embed-text-v1.5 (768d) as the active embedder — no API keys, all-local.
environment._NOTE_RETRY_TUNING: Bulk re-embed retry tuning: raised pending cap + max-age + faster batch/interval so a large embedding backlog drains instead of being parked as failed (defaults park excess/old pending: cap 1000, 24h age, 5-per-5min).
environment._NOTE_SOCKET: macOS sun_path limit (104 chars): the default bridge socket path under database/daemon-ipc.sock exceeds the limit and the launcher fails with EINVAL. Pin to a short /tmp path so the multi-client bridge can bind.

environment._NOTE_1_DB: Database lives at .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database/skill-graph.sqlite by default; MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR overrides, with SYSTEM_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR as a legacy fallback (both still recognized by the launcher + handlers).
environment._NOTE_3_THRESHOLDS: Advisor confidence/uncertainty thresholds are tunable: SPECKIT_ADVISOR_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD (default 0.8), SPECKIT_ADVISOR_UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD (default 0.35). Lane weights tunable via SPECKIT_ADVISOR_LANE_WEIGHTS_JSON + SPECKIT_ADVISOR_LANE_SHADOW_WEIGHTS_JSON (partial-merge JSON, post-022/011/4b).
environment._NOTE_DOC_TRIGGERS: Opt-in doc-frontmatter harvest: skill_graph_scan ingests references/assets trigger_phrases into skill_docs and advisor_recommend returns matchedDocs pointers (dampened derived-lane signal). Default-off in code; enabled for this repo. Unset/false to roll back.
environment._NOTE_HF_EMBED_SOCKET: Shared hf-local model-server socket — identical to mk-spec-memory's so both services share ONE resident nomic server. (skill-advisor only spawns it when SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_MODEL_SERVER_ENABLED=1; otherwise it just connects to mk-spec-memory's.)
environment._NOTE_TRUST_DEFAULT: Daemon-owner trust grant for mutation tools (advisor_rebuild, skill_graph_scan, skill_graph_propagate_enhances). Callers whose _meta omits transport/trust markers default to UNTRUSTED unless this is 'trusted'. Native MCP clients (e.g. /doctor repair flows) send no _meta, so this grant keeps them working; remove it to fail-closed on every transport-absent caller. Explicit _meta trusted:false / callerAuthority:'untrusted' is always rejected.

environment._NOTE_1_DB: Database lives SKILL-LOCAL at .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite by default (shared across runtimes via the .opencode/skills symlink); SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR overrides. Former shared location (.opencode/.spec-kit/code-graph/database/) is migrated back on first launcher startup.
environment._NOTE_3_INDEX_DEFAULTS: INDEX_* committed defaults are false (end-user safe). Maintainer mode: set SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true in .env.local (gitignored); the launcher will force all 5 INDEX_* to true at startup. Per-call code_graph_scan args (includeSkills, includeAgents, includeCommands, includeSpecs, includePlugins) still override env for fine-grained control.
environment._NOTE_4_RESCAN_THRESHOLD: SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_SELECTIVE_REINDEX_THRESHOLD (default 50) controls when the launcher switches from selective rescan to full scan. Raise to delay full-scans (faster session-boot, more drift tolerance); lower to keep the DB tighter. Post-commit hook (.opencode/scripts/git-hooks/post-commit) also invalidates the SQLite when commits exceed SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT_REBUILD_THRESHOLD (default 100).
